summary: Implemented most of the fighter afterimage draw path in src/melee/ft/ftafterimage.c, replacing ftCo_800C2600's NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub with GX state setup, afterimage vertex construction, sword/item parameter lookup, curve subdivision using lbVector helpers, and triangle-strip emission. The PR also refined sword attribute layouts in ftMars/types.h and it/items/types.h so the afterimage code could read scale/color/alpha fields with plausible offsets. decomp.dev reported ftCo_800C2FD8 reached 100% and ftCo_800C2600 improved from 0.22% to 88.71%, but there were no human review comments in the slice.

key_files.role: Main decompilation work: ftCo_800C2600 afterimage rendering implementation and ftCo_800C2FD8 assertion/control-flow adjustment.
Expanded SwordAttrs from opaque padding into f32 and u8 fields used by the afterimage parameter interpretation.
Changed itSword_UnkBytes offsets +0 and +4 from individual bytes to f32 fields, aligning item sword data with afterimage scale usage.

what_changed: Replaced ftCo_800C2600's NOT_IMPLEMENTED with a large partial/mostly matching implementation for drawing fighter afterimages.
Added local AfterimageVtx with position floats and RGBA bytes for the generated GX vertex buffer.
Added AFTERIMAGE_ANGLE_STEP constant set to 0.0872664600610733f for angle-based subdivision between stored afterimage directions.

Configured GX state for translucent, untextured vertex-color rendering: color update on, alpha update off, blend mode src alpha/inv src alpha, alpha compare, z mode, TEV pass color, one color channel, and no culling.
Looked up afterimage parameter data either from a held sword item via it_80285300 when fp->x2101_bits_8 is set, or from fighter dat_attrs for Link/Young Link and Marth/Roy style sword users.

Preserved a switch over many non-sword fighter kinds that simply break in the parameter lookup path, while Link/CLINK use ftLk_DatAttrs x64 and Mars/Emblem use MarsAttributes x78 cast to itSword_UnkBytes.
Computed cumulative distances over the fighter x20B0 ring buffer using x2101_bits_0to6 and x2100, with fallback totalDist = 1.0f for tiny movement.
Generated paired inner/outer vertices using params->x0/x4 scale fields, params->x8/x9 alpha range, and params->xA/xB/xC plus xE/xF/x10 color bytes.
Inserted intermediate vertices when adjacent direction vectors have a valid normalized cross product, using lbVector_Angle and lbVector_RotateAboutUnitAxis for smooth afterimage curvature.
Emitted the generated vertices through GXBegin(GX_TRIANGLESTRIP, GX_VTXFMT0, numVerts + 1), including a manually unrolled loop processing four vertices at a time plus a remainder path.
Invalidated HSD state at the end with HSD_StateInvalidate(-1).
Changed ftCo_800C2FD8's item-kind check from if/else OSReport plus __assert to a switch on itGetKind, using HSD_ASSERTREPORT for the default non-sword case.
decomp.dev reported ftCo_800C2FD8 became a new full match, improving from 98.99% to 100.00%.
decomp.dev reported ftCo_800C2600 improved from 0.22% to 88.71%, and .sdata2 improved from 0.00% to 87.50%.
smart_moves: The PR aligned item and fighter sword attribute structs around the same observed layout: f32 fields at +0 and +4 followed by byte-sized alpha/color fields, allowing the same itSword_UnkBytes interpretation for held sword items and fighter dat_attrs.
Using casts from Link and Marth/Roy dat_attrs to itSword_UnkBytes avoided duplicating the afterimage rendering logic for character-owned sword attributes and item-owned sword attributes.
The implementation preserved low-level GX setup and direct vertex submission patterns, which are often necessary for matching rendering code rather than replacing it with higher-level abstractions.
The ring-buffer traversal explicitly handles wraparound at index 0 back to 2, matching the apparent three-entry afterimage history stored in Fighter.x20B0.
The vertex emission loop includes a four-at-a-time unrolled path plus a remainder label, a common decomp tactic when the compiler generated partially unrolled loops.
Replacing OSReport plus __assert with HSD_ASSERTREPORT in ftCo_800C2FD8 matched enough control/data shape to finish that function according to the bot report.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling rendering effects, struct recovery and function matching are tightly coupled: ftCo_800C2600 needed meaningful sword parameter field widths before scale, alpha, and color math could be expressed correctly.
Opaque byte padding may hide f32 fields; here itSword_UnkBytes +0/+4 and SwordAttrs +0/+4 became f32s because the code uses them in floating-point interpolation.
Do not over-generalize item/fighter attribute layouts too early, but shared casts can be useful when the same binary routine treats several sources as an equivalent parameter block.
GX matching often requires reproducing exact state setup order and direct GXPosition/GXColor emission rather than only matching the visual behavior.
For history/ribbon effects, preserve circular-buffer index arithmetic and signed/unsigned casts around small state fields such as x2100 and x2101 bitfields.

Vector helper calls such as lbVector_CrossprodNormalized, lbVector_Angle, and lbVector_RotateAboutUnitAxis can encode high-level geometric intent while still matching if the surrounding temporaries and loop structure are shaped carefully.
A small adjacent cleanup in an already-near-matching function can yield a full match; in this PR ftCo_800C2FD8 reached 100% while ftCo_800C2600 remained partial.
naming_conventions: Temporary recovered struct fields keep offset-based names such as x0, x4, x8, xA, x10, x14, x18, and x1C until semantic names are known.
Padding remains offset-named, e.g. pad_x11[3], after replacing the earlier pad_x0[0x14] with typed fields.
Local helper type AfterimageVtx uses a semantic name while its members are simple component names x, y, z, r, g, b, a.
The macro AFTERIMAGE_ANGLE_STEP is named for the effect and constant role rather than the raw literal.
Existing project symbol conventions are retained: ftCo_800C2600, ftCo_800C2FD8, it_80285300, it_80285314, lbVector_CrossprodNormalized, and FTKIND_* enums.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use exact primitive GX setup calls in apparent binary order to match render-state side effects.
Represent vertex data with a stack buffer of structs matching direct GX output fields, here AfterimageVtx vtx_buf[152].
Preserve explicit signed casts such as (s8)(u8)fp->x2100 where the original code likely used byte-sized state.
Use manual ring-buffer wraparound conditionals instead of modulo arithmetic when the disassembly suggests branches around a three-slot buffer.
Keep nested scopes and many short-lived locals to influence register allocation and lifetime in a large rendering function.
Use goto-style remainder handling for an unrolled loop when matching compiler output for direct GX vertex submission.
Use HSD_ASSERTREPORT with observed line numbers/messages instead of semantically equivalent OSReport/__assert sequences when that matches the binary.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code improved to 62.56% overall for GALE01, +0.01% and +436 bytes.
Reported 1 new match: main/melee/ft/ftafterimage ftCo_800C2FD8 improved from 98.99% to 100.00%.
Reported unmatched improvements: ftCo_800C2600 from 0.22% to 88.71%, and .sdata2 from 0.00% to 87.50%.
No human review comments or human PR-body rationale were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish ftCo_800C2600 from 88.71% to 100%.
Search for semantic renames of itSword_UnkBytes fields x0, x4, x8, x9, xA, xB, xC, xE, xF, and x10.
Check whether SwordAttrs in ftMars/types.h and itSword_UnkBytes in it/items/types.h remain layout-compatible after later header cleanup.
Look for other afterimage users or fighter ribbon effects that reuse fp->x20B0, x20F8, x20FC, x2100, and x2101 bitfields.
Search future PRs for HSD_ASSERTREPORT replacements that help match assertion-heavy code.
